headline: AI boom and geopolitical relief drive broad bullish consensus across all models
summary: All five AI models converged on broad market optimism for March 31st, 2026, driven by Iran de-escalation hopes, NVIDIA's strategic $2B investment in Marvell, and strong tech sector momentum. SPY received unanimous bullish calls across Claude, GPT-4o, Grok, and Perplexity, though with varying targets (600-645), while AAPL and QQQ also attracted multi-model agreement on upside potential. However, actual closing prices remain unavailable, preventing evaluation of prediction accuracy.

consensus_picks.note: Four models unanimously bullish with targets ranging from 600 to 645, citing geopolitical relief and strong earnings momentum.
Two models aligned on upside with targets of 180-230 based on earnings beat and positive sentiment.
Claude and Grok both bullish on AI chip strength, though with vastly different targets (175 vs 950).
Both models saw tech rally continuation from NVDA-Marvell partnership and de-escalation tailwinds.

best_call.summary: Claude's 85% confidence call on Marvell riding NVIDIA's $2B partnership announcement with an 11% premarket surge was the highest-conviction prediction of the day, though outcome remains pending.

worst_call.summary: Grok's wildly aggressive $950 target for NVDA appears detached from near-term reality and shows potential target-setting overexuberance despite 60% confidence.
